title: Expressing support for designation of the month of April 2025 as "Parkinsons Awareness Month".
plain_summary: This bill asks Congress to make April 2025 a special month to raise awareness about Parkinson's disease. Parkinson's is a serious illness that affects more than one million Americans. The bill recognizes how much this disease impacts patients and their families. It also supports continued research to find better treatments and hopefully a cure for Parkinson's disease.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for making April two thousand twenty-five Parkinson's Awareness Month.

sections.title: Facts About Parkinson's Disease
sections.text: Parkinson's disease affects more than one million people in the United States. Almost ninety thousand people are diagnosed with it each year. It is the fastest growing brain disease in the world and the second most common brain disease that gets worse over time. Scientists believe the disease is caused by a mix of genetic factors (things passed down in families) and environmental factors (things in our surroundings), but they still do not know the exact cause for most people. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Parkinson's disease is the fifteenth leading cause of death in the United States.

sections.title: Future Impact of Parkinson's Disease
sections.text: Experts estimate that by the year two thousand thirty-seven, the number of people with Parkinson's disease in the United States will almost double. The disease will cost the United States at least eighty billion dollars every year.

sections.title: Symptoms and Effects on Families
sections.text: The symptoms of Parkinson's disease can include memory problems and trouble thinking clearly, shaking, slow movement and stiff muscles, trouble walking and keeping balance, difficulty speaking and swallowing, depression, and many other symptoms. There are millions of family members who take care of people with Parkinson's disease, plus friends and loved ones whose lives are greatly affected by this disease.

sections.title: Need for More Research and Support
sections.text: More research, education, and community support services are needed to find better treatments and a cure for Parkinson's disease. These efforts are also needed to help people living with the disease today maintain their dignity.

sections.title: What the House of Representatives Resolves
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports making April Parkinson's Awareness Month and supports the goals and ideas behind this awareness month. The House continues to support research to find better treatments and a cure for Parkinson's disease. The House recognizes the people living with Parkinson's disease who participate in important medical research studies that help advance knowledge about the disease. The House also praises the hard work of organizations, volunteers, researchers, and millions of people across the country who are working to improve the quality of life for people living with Parkinson's disease and their families.
